item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Rise on Oil Price Gain, Lingering Cold
item.description: US natural gas futures rose on a cold shift in weather forecasts, indicating higher demand for the heating and power-plant fuel even as the gas market’s so-called winter heating season recedes in the rearview mirror. Rising oil prices, which have influenced the front-month US gas contract in recent months, may also be supporting US gas prices by driving financial inflows into baskets of energy products.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Expects Little Change to Underlying Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said his outlook for underlying price pressures in the US was largely unchanged despite his expectation that higher energy costs stemming from the war in Iran will boost overall inflation.

item.title: Tishman Speyer Steps Toward Regaining Control of Chrysler Building
item.description: A former owner of Manhattan’s Chrysler Building is one step closer to regaining control of the iconic Art Deco skyscraper.

item.title: US Core Capital Goods Orders Rose Ahead of Middle East War
item.description: US orders for business equipment rebounded in February, suggesting companies were moving forward on investment plans before the Iran war.

item.title: Dynamic Prices May Lift UK Inflation Fears, Bank of England Says
item.description: Dynamic pricing by UK companies could ramp up inflation fears but also boost the economy by allowing firms to use their capacity more efficiently, according to the Bank of England.

item.title: Brazil Courts Investors Ahead of Potential Euro Bond Return
item.description: Brazil is sounding out investors as it weighs a return to global debt markets, potentially paving the way for its first euro-denominated issuance in more than a decade.

item.title: McEwen Copper in Talks With Lenders to Fund Argentina Project
item.description: McEwen Copper Inc. is in talks with global lenders as it seeks financing for its $4 billion Los Azules project in Argentina, a potential step toward bringing one of the country’s largest undeveloped deposits into production.

item.title: Stock Futures Fall Ahead of Iran Deadline; Universal Music Soars
item.description: US stocks are falling in premarket trading as mixed economic data and geopolitical tensions weigh on investors ahead of a key US deadline on Iran.

item.title: Argentina Looks to Global Debt Markets to Finance Energy Boom
item.description: Argentina’s corporate borrowers are turning to global debt markets with a different goal this year — funding an energy-driven expansion rather than patching up balance sheets battered by years of crisis.

item.title: Supersonic Fighter Jet Startup Becomes Unicorn With New Funding
item.description: Hypersonic plane startup Hermeus Corp. raised $350 million in a funding round to scale up its efforts to build the fastest uncrewed aircraft ever for the US Department of Defense.

item.title: BlackRock, State Street Target Invesco’s $379 Billion Tech Grip
item.description: Wall Street’s biggest ETF issuers are circling Invesco Ltd.’s Nasdaq 100 franchise, threatening to end its near-exclusive hold on the index.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Broadcom, CVS, Estée Lauder, Wingstop
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders await President Donald Trump’s Tuesday evening deadline for Iran to make a peace deal.

item.title: UK Signals It Won’t Let US Use Bases for Strikes on Iran Energy
item.description: The UK government signaled it won’t let the US use British military bases for strikes on Iranian energy or civilian infrastructure after President Donald Trump threatened an escalation of operations against Tehran.

item.title: Mozambique Dollar Bonds Slump to 2023 Low on Restructure Plan
item.description: Mozambique’s dollar bonds fell to the lowest in almost three years after authorities gave the strongest signal yet of plans to seek restructuring talks with creditors.

item.title: Bond Traders Risk Being Wrongfooted by 2022 Playbook, UBS Warns
item.description: Traders risk being wrongfooted by bets that major central banks will move in unison to respond to the threat of prolonged war in Iran, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Boosts Tiny Space ETF With Record Surge in Inflows
item.description: The looming market debut of Elon Musk’s rocket company SpaceX has sparked a rush of capital into smaller space ventures with investors eager to find a way to hitch a ride onto what could be the biggest listing ever.

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz Deadline Lays Bare Scant Options
item.description: Iran is tightening control over the strategic waterway even as the US demands a reopening.

item.title: US Inflation Shows Worrying Parallels With 2022 Price Surge
item.description: The 1970s and early 80s showed what high US inflation looked like. Now the benchmark is 2022.

item.title: War in Iran Drives Russian Oil Prices to a 13-Year High
item.description: Russian crude prices rose to the highest in more than 13 years as Moscow benefited from the Iran-linked global oil rally.

item.title: Google Adds Mental Health Tools to Gemini Chatbot After Lawsuit
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google plans to introduce new mental health support features for its Gemini chatbot as the company and rivals, like OpenAI, have faced several lawsuits accusing their artificial intelligence tools of leading to harm.

item.title: Microsoft Plays Catch-Up in Data Center Build-Out
item.description: The software company slowed spending, but now finds itself falling behind

item.title: Cerebras Backer Eclipse Raises $1.3 Billion for Robotics, AI Infrastructure
item.description: Venture capital firm Eclipse has raised $1.3 billion to invest in startups working in physical industries such as artificial intelligence infrastructure, manufacturing and defense.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s Hottest AI Metric Is Also Its Least Trusted
item.description: The co-founder of Cluely — an Andreessen Horowitz-backed startup with the motto, “Cheat on everything” — ignited a controversy earlier this month after confessing to lying to a reporter about his company’s performance on a popular Silicon Valley metric: annual recurring revenue, or ARR.

item.title: Blackstone Hits $10 Billion Cap for Opportunistic Credit Fund
item.description: Blackstone Inc. raised $10 billion for its latest opportunistic credit fund, signaling sustained appetite from institutional investors to capitalize on upheaval in the private debt market.

item.title: US Supply Chains Are Straining Like It’s 2022 Again
item.description: Transport costs are rising as US diesel prices soar amid five weeks of war in the Mideast

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau Leads Investor Group to Acquire Sportsbox AI
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau is spearheading an investor group that is acquiring Sportsbox AI, a company specializing in AI technology and 3D motion capture for mobile sports and fitness coaching and training applications. Terms of the deal were not disclosed but DeChambeau said the transaction is worth eight figures.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Prioritizes Stability as Rupiah Hits Record Lows
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank is continuing to intervene in the markets with stability its “top priority” following the currency’s three-day slide to multiple record lows against the dollar.

item.title: Trafigura Fraudster Gupta Linked to Mauritian Bank Collapse
item.description: The businessman who masterminded a huge nickel fraud against Trafigura Group was named by Mauritius as the ultimate beneficiary of toxic loans that led to the collapse of a local lender.

item.title: Poland Returns to Foreign Debt Markets With Dollar Bond Offer
item.description: Poland is the latest emerging-market sovereign to return to international bond markets since the start of war in Iran, offering a three-tranche, dollar-denominated debt issue.

item.title: Another Trump Ultimatum Leaves Investors Grasping for Direction
item.description: Stock-index futures erase losses as latest turning point looms
